$comment: Phase 1 country baseline for Kazakhstan (run-kz-2026-07-14). Every factual statement links to evidence-ledger evidence_ids. Detailed maps: country-regulatory-map.json / country-payment-map.json / country-language-map.json; all sources in country-source-map.json.

legal_status.summary: Crypto is legal and actively regulated. Law No. 193-VII 'On Digital Assets' (eff. 2023-04-01, amended 2026-01-16) plus a comprehensive licensing framework in force since 2026-05-01. Organising circulation of digital assets without a licence is prohibited (Art. 11(5)); trading profits on LICENSED platforms are income-tax-exempt per presidential decree of 2026-07-07.

market_risks.summary: Active enforcement against unlicensed platforms: 1,100+ blocked in the year to Jan 2026; AFM AML seizures; AFSA 2026-04-29 warning explicitly names OKX, HTX, Bitget and MEXC as unlicensed toward Kazakhstan citizens. For CBW this puts three of six candidate exchanges under a Gate 02 regulator-warning trigger.

marketing_restrictions.summary: The AFSA warning targets platforms that 'advertise or promote their services to citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan' without authorisation — promotion of unlicensed platforms to KZ users is itself the regulator's stated concern. No dedicated affiliate-marketing statute captured yet.
marketing_restrictions.open_question: Whether third-party affiliate promotion (as opposed to the platform's own advertising) is separately restricted — owner-review input for any future KZ page.

banking_p2p.summary: Kaspi and Halyk dominate retail payments; KZT P2P settles mainly via bank transfer to Kaspi/Halyk/Forte/Jusan/BCC/Freedom. The only verified regulated KZT P2P among the six candidates is Bybit Kazakhstan (Halyk, Nov 2025).

mobile_environment.summary: UNKNOWN — per-exchange KZ storefront availability not researched yet (kz-t04).
